spec_match.rationale: Uses the canonical `SpeechRecognition.start(track)` overload from the Web Speech API spec and exercises both a raw microphone track and a processed Web Audio output track.

interactive.rationale: The page starts real getUserMedia capture, builds a Web Audio graph, updates raw and processed VU meters, and renders live SpeechRecognition output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now promises raw microphone and processed Web Audio track input, and both source modes are available in the demo.

edge_cases.rationale: The recognizer catches `start(track)` failures with a visible fallback, exposes track-ended handling, and has a control that simulates the selected track ending.

references.rationale: The page links the canonical ChromeStatus entry https://chromestatus.com/feature/5178378197139456.

design_system.rationale: The updated CSS uses shared variables, explicit button and pill color pairs, responsive grids, and no static-card pattern.

summary: The live bench now matches the spec shape, cites the correct ChromeStatus ID, demonstrates a processed Web Audio source, and surfaces track-ended cleanup.
